mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding corporate revenues and consumer demand capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for PAYEMS 1-month momentum is below 0.02 over any 2-year window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of top-quartile PAYEMS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above the zero lower bound signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and debt service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%, positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return in triggered periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales concurrent with falling industrial production create a demand-supply mismatch that squeezes corporate margins through inventory depletion costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and INDPRO growth shows positive correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC > 0) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds structural levels, accelerating payroll growth signals a recovery in labor income and aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon that would be absent in full-employment regimes.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, top-decile PAYEMS growth months fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or the conditional mean return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show a negative Rank IC with 5-day forward returns (IC < 0) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below replacement-level demand signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream durable goods consumption, which reduces aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In months when HOUST is below 1000, positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently elevated consumer price inflation and high unemployment define stagflation, which erodes corporate pricing power and consumer real spending simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Jointly high levels of CPIAUCSL and UNRATE produce positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term fails to show negative predictive power (Rank IC > 0) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative real federal funds rate, calculated as the nominal rate minus prevailing inflation, drives capital out of fixed income and into equities in a search for yield, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Periods where the real federal funds rate is negative fail to generate higher 20-day forward returns than periods with positive real rates, or the difference is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).
